qa_pairs.question: What is the command to create an SSH tunnel from a desktop to a PostgreSQL database server on the Cedar cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: To establish an SSH tunnel from your desktop to a PostgreSQL database server on the Cedar cluster, you can use the command: `ssh -L PORT:cedar-pgsql-vm.int.cedar.computecanada.ca:5432 someuser@fir.alliancecan.ca`. In this command, `PORT` is a chosen local port number on your desktop, `cedar-pgsql-vm.int.cedar.computecanada.ca:5432` specifies the target PostgreSQL server and its port, and `someuser@fir.alliancecan.ca` represents your username for connecting to the Fir gateway cluster.

qa_pairs.question: How do you set up an SSH tunnel from a desktop to a MySQL database server on the Cedar cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: You can create an SSH tunnel from your desktop to a MySQL database server on the Cedar cluster using the command: `ssh -L PORT:cedar-mysql-vm.int.cedar.computecanada.ca:3306 someuser@fir.alliancecan.ca`. In this command, replace `PORT` with your desired local port number (which should not be greater than 32768) and `someuser` with your actual account username. This tunnel connects through `fir.alliancecan.ca`.
